channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Chanchalguda Jail Museum Opening on April 28 in Telangana
item.description: The Telangana Prisons Department is all set to inaugurate a unique jail museum at the historic Chanchalguda Central Prison in Hyderabad from April 28. This initiative aims to showcase the rich history...

item.title: Government seeks report after oil tanker with Pakistani crew reportedly hijacked near Somalia
item.description: Federal Minister for Maritime Affairs Junaid Anwar Chaudhry has formally sought a detailed report regarding reports of an oil tanker hijacking near the Somali coast, where several Pakistani crew membe...

item.title: Tokyo public servants adopt casual summer attire to reduce energy use amid rising heat and oil concerns
item.description: Tokyo metropolitan government officials are encouraging public servants to swap their traditional suits for shorts this summer, in a move aimed at reducing energy consumption and keeping staff comfort...

item.title: Voice actor Rupert Degas uses satire to critique Anthony Albanese’s leadership style
item.description: Veteran voice actor Rupert Degas, known for his work across children’s television, audiobooks and advertising, has turned his talents to political satire, creating a widely followed impersonation of P...

item.title: Trump's $1 Million Gold Card Shows Minimal Financial Impact on U.S. Treasury
item.description: Donald Trump's $1 million gold card, reportedly intended to generate dividends for the U.S. Treasury, has so far contributed minimally. According to available reports, only a single approval has been ...

item.title: Tanzania Premier League Derby Between Yanga and Simba Moved to Isamuhyo Stadium
item.description: The Tanzania Premier League Board has officially shifted the highly anticipated Kariakoo Derby between Young Africans (Yanga) and Simba from Benjamin Mkapa Stadium to Meja Jenerali Isamuhyo Stadium. T...

item.title: Gauteng sees rise in malaria cases, but most are imported
item.description: Gauteng has recently experienced a spike in malaria cases, a disease typically not found in the province. Between January and March 2026, 414 confirmed malaria cases were reported, with 11 deaths. Whi...

item.title: Key Durban weekend events: Drakensberg Boys Choir concerts, gospel picnic and Amanzimtoti Surf Fest
item.description: Durban and surrounding areas are set for a lively long weekend with a mix of music, faith-based gatherings and coastal sport. One of the standout events is the Drakensberg Boys Choir’s “Music in the M...

item.title: SIU hails court ruling to recover R85m from failed Mozambique border wall project
item.description: The Special Investigating Unit (SIU) has welcomed a recent ruling by the Special Tribunal that nullifies the R85 million tender awarded to ISF Shula Joint Venture for constructing a concrete barrier a...

item.title: Temporary Water Suspension in Karachi Due to Maintenance at Dhabeji Pumping Station
item.description: KARACHI: The Karachi Water and Sewerage Corporation (KWSC) has announced a temporary suspension of water supply in various areas of Karachi due to maintenance work at the Dhabeji Pumping Station. This...

item.title: WHO-Led Big Catch-Up Vaccination Reaches Millions of Previously Unvaccinated Children
item.description: A major global immunisation drive led by WHO has successfully delivered over 100 million vaccine doses to children around the world, making it the largest catch-up effort in history aimed at reversing...

item.title: Ogun State opens new Summit Household factory in Ota to boost manufacturing and jobs
item.description: Ogun State government don officially commission one ultra-modern manufacturing factory owned by Summit Household Solutions Limited for Ota, further strengthening the state reputation as one of Nigeria...

item.title: Alankode Leelakrishnan to contest Kerala Assembly elections from Thrissur as CPI candidate
item.description: In a notable development ahead of the 2026 Kerala Assembly elections, Alankode Leelakrishnan, a renowned poet and cultural activist, has been announced as the Communist Party of India (CPI) candidate ...

item.title: NASA’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope Faces Budget Cuts Threatening Key Scientific Missions
item.description: The Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, NASA’s latest astronomical observatory, is poised to revolutionise our understanding of the universe. Designed to study over 100,000 exoplanets, hundreds of mill...

item.title: Exploring Japan's Innovative Food and Drink Vending Culture
item.description: Japan has earned a reputation as a gourmand’s paradise, not just for its high-end restaurants but also for its diverse and convenient food and drink offerings. From ramen and sushi to street food like...

item.title: NMC directs medical colleges to restrict leave ahead of NEET-UG 2026 to ensure exam integrity
item.description: The National Medical Commission (NMC) has issued an advisory asking all medical colleges across India not to grant leave to students on May 2 and 3, 2026, except in exceptional and well-justified case...

item.title: UP Authorities Constitute Special Team to Investigate Minor's Rape and Death Including Hospital Lapses
item.description: Uttar Pradesh authorities have formed a special investigation team to look into the shocking case involving the rape and subsequent murder of a minor girl, which has raised serious concerns over hospi...

item.title: Jamhuri High School Overview: Fees, KCSE Results, Location and Contacts
item.description: Jamhuri High School, founded in 1906, is one of Kenya's oldest and historically significant learning institutions. Originally established as the Government Indian School and later named The Duke of Gl...

item.title: FG Advises Nigerians In South Africa To Stay Safe Amid Rising Xenophobic Violence
item.description: Federal Government, through Nigerians in Diaspora Commission (NiDCOM), don raise serious alarm to Nigerians wey dey South Africa as fresh wave of xenophobic attacks don start again for some parts of t...

item.title: US Justice Department closes investigation into Fed building cost overruns
item.description: The United States Justice Department has decided to close its investigation into cost overruns associated with Federal Reserve building renovations. This investigation, which had attracted considerabl...
